channel.title: Wellfit Jira
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [PAY-3605] 1.1 Implement Unmatched Transactions Alert
item.description:
Summary

Add automated email alerting to the Treasury API's settlement reconciliation workflow. When the create-funding-batch process detects unmatched transactions (eMAF orphans or Payments orphans), the system sends a single aggregate email to the configured Jira intake address, which auto-creates a triage ticket. Clean runs with zero mismatches produce no email. This replaces the existing per-issue warning email with a structured, Jira-optimized format that clearly separates orphan types and includes machine-parseable metadata for downstream automation.

User Story

As an operations team member, I want to receive a single, structured email alert when the Treasury API detects unmatched settlement transactions during eMAF processing, so that a Jira ticket is automatically created for triage without manual monitoring of reconciliation results.

This story delivers the complete alerting capability: orchestrator, content composition, SendGrid integration, telemetry, configuration binding with fail-fast validation, removal of the legacy per-issue email, and full test coverage.

QA Acceptance Criteria

  1. When a settlement processing run detects one or more unmatched transactions, exactly one email is sent containing all mismatches from that run
  2. When a settlement processing run detects zero unmatched transactions, no email is sent
  3. The email subject line follows the format [Treasury Settlement] Unmatched transactions YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC (N total) where N is the total mismatch count
  4. The email body clearly separates eMAF orphans and Payments orphans into distinct labeled sections, each listing TransactionId, Amount, and ReferenceDate
  5. If only one orphan type exists in a run, the empty section is omitted from the body entirely
  6. The email body includes a structured [run-metadata] block at the bottom with batch_id, counts, and schema_version
  7. The email recipient is read from application configuration (notifications:settlement:recipients) — no hardcoded addresses
  8. If email sending fails, the settlement processing run still completes successfully (email failure does not interrupt funding batch processing)
  9. If email sending fails, the failure appears in Application Insights telemetry as a trackable event
  10. The previous per-issue "Unmatched Settlment Records Detected" email is no longer sent — only the new aggregated alert is active
  11. Application startup fails fast if the alert recipient or sender configuration is missing or blank

Business Acceptance Criteria (Non-Technical)

Given the Treasury API processes an eMAF settlement file with 3 eMAF orphans and 1 Payments orphan

When the processing run completes

Then a single email is sent listing all 4 mismatches organized by type

And a Jira ticket is created from the email by the intake mailbox integration

Given the Treasury API processes an eMAF settlement file with zero mismatches

When the processing run completes

Then no email is sent and no Jira ticket is created

Given the email sending fails (e.g., SendGrid outage)

When the processing run encounters the failure

Then the funding batch processing completes normally without interruption

And the failure is recorded for operational visibility

Technical Acceptance Criteria

Given UnmatchedTransaction records exist with TimeStamp >= runStart

When UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator.Send(runStart, batchId) is invoked

Then records are queried via Domain Facade filtered by run-start timestamp

And UnmatchedAlertContent is constructed with eMAF orphans and Payments orphans separated

And IUnmatchedAlertNotifier.Notify(content) is called synchronously

And UnmatchedAlertSent telemetry event is emitted with batch_id, emaf_count, payments_count, total_count, recipient_hash

Given zero UnmatchedTransaction records exist with TimeStamp >= runStart

When UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator.Send(runStart, batchId) is invoked

Then IUnmatchedAlertNotifier.Notify is NOT called

And UnmatchedAlertSuppressed telemetry event is emitted with batch_id

Given IUnmatchedAlertNotifier.Notify throws any exception

When the orchestrator's try/catch catches it

Then Logger.LogError is called with the exception and batch ID

And UnmatchedAlertSendFailed telemetry event is emitted with batch_id, total_count, exception_type, exception_message

And the exception is NOT propagated to CreateFundingBatch

Given UnmatchedAlertOptions.Recipients or UnmatchedAlertOptions.NoReplyEmail is empty/whitespace in configuration

When the Treasury API host starts

Then startup fails fast with OptionsValidationException before processing any requests

Given the orchestrator call is placed inside CreateFundingBatch.cs existing inner try/catch (lines 60–94)

When the settlement processing run executes

Then the orchestrator runs synchronously within that scope

And the existing "Unmatched Settlment Records Detected" email send at lines 67–70 is removed

And the "Funding Rejects Detected" (line 75) and "Issue encountered during funding processing" (line 93) email sends remain untouched

Given a mixed-orphan run produces an email

When UnmatchedAlertContent.BuildSubject() is called

Then the subject matches [Treasury Settlement] Unmatched transactions YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC (N total) using the run-start timestamp at minute precision

Given a mixed-orphan run produces an email

When UnmatchedAlertContent.BuildBody() is called

Then the body contains a header block with run-start timestamp (second precision), funding batch ID, and total counts

And eMAF Orphans section with fixed-width columns (TransactionId=27, Amount=11 right-aligned, ReferenceDate=13)

And Payments Orphans section with the same column layout

And a structured [run-metadata] marker block with schema_version: 1, all fields present in fixed order

Given only eMAF orphans exist (zero Payments orphans)

When BuildBody() is called

Then the Payments Orphans section is fully omitted

And the header still shows 0 Payments orphans in the count line

And the marker block payments_orphan_count is 0

Given Notify(content) is called on the UnmatchedAlertNotifier

When the notifier resolves the recipient from IOptions<UnmatchedAlertOptions>

Then it calls ISendEmail.Send(subject, body, noReplyEmail, recipients) via Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52

And computes SHA-256(recipients.ToLower()) and returns the hash as a string

Technical Requirements

  • Define IUnmatchedAlertNotifier interface in Application/Notifications/ — sync string Notify(UnmatchedAlertContent content) returning recipient_hash
  • Implement UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator in Application/Notifications/ — queries Domain Facade, composes content, invokes notifier, emits telemetry, swallows on failure
  • Implement UnmatchedAlertContent as a C# record in Application/Notifications/ with BuildSubject() and BuildBody() instance methods (pure functions on immutable data)
  • Define UnmatchedAlertOptions POCO in Application/Notifications/ with Recipients and NoReplyEmail properties
  • Implement UnmatchedAlertNotifier in Infrastructure/Notifications/ wrapping ISendEmail from Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52
  • Register orchestrator in TreasuryApplicationFeature.cs; register notifier + options binding + inline fail-fast validation in TreasuryInfrastructureFeature.cs
  • Hook orchestrator call inside existing inner try/catch in CreateFundingBatch.cs (lines 60–94), post-validation
  • Remove existing per-issue email send at CreateFundingBatch.cs lines 67–70 (D3)
  • Email body is plain text only (D7) — requires Wellfit.SendGrid PlainTextContent upgrade as prerequisite
  • Amounts formatted as $###,##0.00; timestamps use run-start time, never DateTime.UtcNow at send time
  • All telemetry properties use snake_case keys; recipient_hash is SHA-256 hex of lowercased recipient
  • Runtime: .NET 6 / Wellfit 2.1.0 — must not introduce .NET 8 dependencies

Requirements Fulfilled

  • FR: FR1, FR2, FR3, FR4, FR5, FR6, FR7, FR8, FR9
  • NFR: NFR1, NFR2, NFR3, NFR4, NFR5

Telemetry Events

Event When Properties
UnmatchedAlertSent After successful send batch_id, emaf_count, payments_count, total_count, recipient_hash
UnmatchedAlertSuppressed Zero mismatches batch_id
UnmatchedAlertSendFailed SendGrid call throws batch_id, total_count, exception_type, exception_message

Architecture Compliance

Naming & Placement

Type Layer Folder Namespace
IUnmatchedAlertNotifier (interface) Application Application/Notifications/ Treasury.Application.Notifications
UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator (class) Application Application/Notifications/ Treasury.Application.Notifications
UnmatchedAlertContent (record) Application Application/Notifications/ Treasury.Application.Notifications
UnmatchedAlertOptions (POCO) Application Application/Notifications/ Treasury.Application.Notifications
UnmatchedAlertNotifier (implementation) Infrastructure Infrastructure/Notifications/ Treasury.Infrastructure.Notifications
  • Orchestrator class: {Subject}AlertOrchestrator — never Service, Manager, Helper, or Sender
    * Interface: I{Subject}

    AlertNotifier — Notifier abstracts the delivery channel

  • Content carrier: {Subject}

    AlertContent — a record with rendering instance methods (BuildSubject(), BuildBody())

  • Do NOT introduce a separate EmailService

Email Content Rendering

  • Amounts: $###,##0.00 (US locale, two decimals, dollar prefix)
  • Counts: bare integers (no thousands separator below 10,000)
  • Subject timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC, minute precision
  • Body header timestamp: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC, second precision
  • Marker block timestamp: full ISO 8601 with Z suffix
  • All timestamps: run's actual start time (captured at LOCK acquisition), never DateTime.UtcNow at send time
  • Fixed-width columns: TransactionId=27, Amount=11 (right-aligned), ReferenceDate=13
  • Header underline row, NOT Markdown pipe tables, NOT tab-separated
  • Empty section (eMAF or Payments Orphans): fully omitted from body; header still shows true counts

Structured Marker Block

Exact format — strict contract, all fields always present, fixed order:

---
[run-metadata]
schema_version: 1
batch_id: {FundingBatch.Id}
run_started_utc: {ISO 8601 with Z}
emaf_orphan_count: {int}
payments_orphan_count: {int}
total_unmatched: {int}
alert_source: treasury-api/unmatched-settlement
---

Keys: snake_case, values: bare (unquoted), no comments, no JSON/YAML/TOML.

Error Handling

  • Single try/catch at orchestrator level only — notifier does NOT catch
  • Catch Exception (not specific types) — NFR5 requires funding run must never fail
  • Always log AND track telemetry on failure
  • Never re-throw, never wrap, never return result object
  • Sync API: Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52 exposes void Send(...) only — no SendAsync

Configuration

  • IOptions<UnmatchedAlertOptions> injected in Notifier (Infrastructure), NOT in orchestrator
  • Bound from existing notifications:settlement:recipients and notifications:noReplyEmail — no new keys
  • DI registration in TreasuryInfrastructureFeature.cs via [Feature] attribute pattern
  • Fail-fast: AddOptions<UnmatchedAlertOptions>().Configure<IConfiguration>(...).Validate(o => !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(o.Recipients) && !string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(o.NoReplyEmail), "...").ValidateOnStart()
  • No separate IValidateOptions<> class — inline lambda only

Testing

Category Location Class Name
Orchestrator unit tests Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/ UnmatchedAlertOrchestratorTests
Content + rendering tests Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/ UnmatchedAlertContentTests
Notifier unit tests Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/ UnmatchedAlertNotifierTests

Mandatory test coverage:

  • Clean run (zero mismatches) → notifier NOT called → UnmatchedAlertSuppressed tracked
  • Mixed run (eMAF + Payments) → both sections present, marker counts correct
  • eMAF-only run → Payments section omitted; marker counts reflect zero
  • Payments-only run → eMAF section omitted
  • Notifier throws → orchestrator does NOT propagate; UnmatchedAlertSendFailed tracked; logger called
  • Subject contains run timestamp at minute precision, UTC
  • Body header timestamps use run-start time
  • Marker block schema (all keys, fixed order, schema_version: 1)

Mocks:

  • Moq 4.20.72 (existing Treasury API test stack)
  • Mock IUnmatchedAlertNotifier in orchestrator tests — never ISendEmail directly
  • Mock ISendEmail in notifier tests — never SendGridClient directly
  • Mock Domain Facade query in orchestrator tests
  • UnmatchedAlertContentTests: instantiate record directly, no mocks — pure functions

Tasks / Subtasks

Task 1: Create Application-layer contracts and content

  • Create Application/Notifications/IUnmatchedAlertNotifier.cs — interface with string Notify(UnmatchedAlertContent content)
  • Create Application/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertContent.cs — record with BuildSubject() and BuildBody() instance methods
  • Create Application/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertOptions.cs — POCO with Recipients and NoReplyEmail
  • Verify BuildSubject() produces [Treasury Settlement] Unmatched transactions YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM UTC (N total)
  • Verify BuildBody() renders fixed-width columns, section omission, and marker block per architecture

Task 2: Create orchestrator

  • Create Application/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator.cs
  • Inject IUnmatchedAlertNotifier, Domain Facade, ILogger<>, TelemetryClient
  • Implement Send(DateTime runStart, Guid batchId) — query UnmatchedTransaction filtered by TimeStamp >= runStart
  • Gate send by total > 0; emit UnmatchedAlertSuppressed on zero mismatches
  • try/catch boundary: invoke notifier, emit UnmatchedAlertSent on success, swallow and emit UnmatchedAlertSendFailed on failure

Task 3: Create Infrastructure-layer notifier

  • Create Infrastructure/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertNotifier.cs
  • Inject ISendEmail (Wellfit.SendGrid) and IOptions<UnmatchedAlertOptions>
  • Implement string Notify(UnmatchedAlertContent content) — calls _email.Send(subject, body, noReplyEmail, recipients), computes SHA-256 of lowercased recipients, returns hash
  • Let exceptions propagate (no catch in notifier)

Task 4: DI registration and config binding

  • Register UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator in TreasuryApplicationFeature.cs
  • Register IUnmatchedAlertNotifier → UnmatchedAlertNotifier in TreasuryInfrastructureFeature.cs
  • Bind UnmatchedAlertOptions from notifications:settlement:recipients and notifications:noReplyEmail
  • Add inline fail-fast validation: .Validate(...).ValidateOnStart()

Task 5: Hook into CreateFundingBatch and remove legacy email

  • Inject UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator into CreateFundingBatch constructor
  • Call orchestrator.Send(runStart, batchId) INSIDE the existing inner try/catch (lines 60–94), post-validation
  • Capture runStart at LOCK acquisition time
  • Remove existing per-issue email send at lines 67–70 ONLY ("Unmatched Settlment Records Detected")
  • Verify lines 75 and 93 email sends remain untouched

Task 6: Unit tests

  • Create Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertContentTests.cs — subject format, mixed/eMAF-only/Payments-only body, marker block, amount formatting, timestamps
  • Create Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertOrchestratorTests.cs — clean run, mixed run, notifier throws
  • Create Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/UnmatchedAlertNotifierTests.cs — ISendEmail.Send called correctly, SHA-256 hash returned

Dev Notes

Critical Constraints

  • Runtime: .NET 6 / Wellfit 2.1.0 — must NOT pull .NET 8 dependencies
  • Wellfit.SendGrid version in Treasury API: 2.0.0.52 (not 2.1.0 as listed in boot snapshot)
  • Sync API only: Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52 exposes void Send(...) — no SendAsync, no CancellationToken
  • NFR5 invariant: Orchestrator call MUST be inside CreateFundingBatch.cs existing inner try/catch (lines 60–94) — the async void Receive() pattern means exceptions escaping that scope silently vanish
  • D3 scope: Remove ONLY lines 67–70. Lines 75 ("Funding Rejects Detected") and 93 ("Issue encountered during funding processing") are OUT OF SCOPE
  • Semicolon-delimited recipients: Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52 splits on ; (not comma) at SendGridEmailSender.cs line 74

Architecture Anti-Patterns (DO NOT)

  • Do NOT create a separate EmailService, ContentRenderer, or SendResult class
  • Do NOT use Markdown pipe tables or tab-separated values in email body
  • Do NOT emit a single UnmatchedAlertOutcome event — must be three distinct events
  • Do NOT use IConfiguration["key"] inline — bind via IOptions<> at startup
  • Do NOT inject IOptions<UnmatchedAlertOptions> into the orchestrator — only the Notifier consumes it
  • Do NOT catch exceptions in the Notifier — let them propagate to the orchestrator
  • Do NOT use DateTime.UtcNow at send time for timestamps — use run-start time from LOCK acquisition
  • Do NOT rename Application/Intrerfaces/ (pre-existing typo, out of scope)
  • Do NOT create a Tests/InfrastructureTests/ project — it doesn't exist in the .sln

Project Structure (Delta)

treasury-api/
├── Application/
│   ├── Features/TreasuryApplicationFeature.cs               MOD
│   └── Notifications/                                       NEW
│       ├── IUnmatchedAlertNotifier.cs                       NEW
│       ├── UnmatchedAlertOrchestrator.cs                    NEW
│       ├── UnmatchedAlertContent.cs                         NEW
│       └── UnmatchedAlertOptions.cs                         NEW
├── Infrastructure/
│   ├── Features/TreasuryInfrastructureFeature.cs            MOD
│   └── Notifications/                                       NEW
│       └── UnmatchedAlertNotifier.cs                        NEW
├── Application/Endpoints/Funding/CreateFundingBatch/
│   └── CreateFundingBatch.cs                                MOD
└── Tests/ApplicationTests/
    └── Notifications/                                       NEW
        ├── UnmatchedAlertOrchestratorTests.cs               NEW
        ├── UnmatchedAlertContentTests.cs                    NEW
        └── UnmatchedAlertNotifierTests.cs                   NEW

Vibe Code

  • CreateFundingBatch.cs lines 60–94 — existing inner try/catch where orchestrator hooks in; lines 67–70 are the per-issue email send being replaced
  • CreateFundingBatch.cs line 51 — existing inline IConfiguration read for notifications:noReplyEmail
  • CreateFundingBatch.cs line 66 — existing _domainFacade.Find<UnmatchedTransaction>().Any() (the unfiltered boolean check this story replaces with timestamp-filtered query)
  • Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52 SendGridEmailSender.cs line 74 — semicolon-delimited recipient splitting
  • Wellfit.SendGrid 2.0.0.52 SendGridEmailSender.cs line 88 — currently sets only HtmlContent (PlainTextContent upgrade needed)
  • Infrastructure/Features/TreasuryInfrastructureFeature.cs — [Feature] attribute pattern for DI registration
  • Application/Features/TreasuryApplicationFeature.cs — Application-layer service registration

Notes for AI Agent

  • BLOCKER: Verify Wellfit.SendGrid PlainTextContent upgrade has shipped BEFORE starting implementation — if not available, escalate and block
  • The existing async void Receive() pattern in CreateFundingBatch.cs is a known risk; the orchestrator call is synchronous and MUST remain inside the inner try/catch (lines 60–94) — never after
  • UnmatchedTransaction records may or may not be cleared between runs (not documented); the run-start timestamp filter is therefore load-bearing, not just defensive
  • The Application/Intrerfaces/ folder has a pre-existing typo — do NOT rename it, it is out of scope
  • No Tests/InfrastructureTests/ project exists in Treasury API.sln — all tests go in Tests/ApplicationTests/Notifications/
  • No typed-options precedent exists in Treasury API; the IOptions<UnmatchedAlertOptions> + inline lambda validation is a deliberate improvement over existing inline IConfiguration reads
  • Test naming convention: verify against existing tests in Tests/ApplicationTests/ before authoring — match whatever pattern is found (expected: MethodUnderTest_StateUnderTest_ExpectedBehavior)
  • The two-phase approach (Story A additive + Story B removal) from the architecture is combined into this single story per product owner direction — implement additively first, verify, then remove the old send path, all within one PR
  • D3 removes ONLY the "Unmatched Settlment Records Detected" send at lines 67–70 (note pre-existing typo "Settlment"). The two other email sends in the same file are OUT OF SCOPE and must remain unchanged

Blocker

Blocked by: Wellfit.SendGrid library upgrade adding PlainTextContent support (external dependency — framework team)

Relations

  • Blocks: None
  • Blocked by: Wellfit.SendGrid library upgrade adding PlainTextContent support (external dependency — framework team)
  • Related to: None

Trunk Readiness

Assessment: GREEN

All tasks produce atomic, non-breaking changes when merged in order. The additive phase (new orchestrator, notifier, content, options, DI registration) introduces no behavioral change until the hook-point task wires it in. The destructive removal (D3 — 3 lines) happens in the same PR after the new path is verified. No feature flags required — the new code is inert until wired. Safe for trunk-based merge.

BMAD Reference

Field Value
Product payments
Feature unmatched-settlement-transaction-alerts
Epic 1 — Automated Unmatched Settlement Transaction Alerts ( PAY-3602 IN-DEVELOPMENT )
Story 1.1

Dev command: /wellfit-dev --product pay --feature unmatched-settlement-transaction-alerts --story 1.1

comments.comment:
Swetha Bayya I am pushing to Completed since shows no QA Required
If that needs to change, just pop back to In QA

Discussion at Stand Up is that this will be caught in later overall tsting

PAY-3605-Story-1.1-E2E-Unmatched-Settlement-Alert-Runbook.md
PAY-3605-Unmatched-Settlement-Alert-DEV.postman_environment.json
PAY-3605-Unmatched-Settlement-Alert.postman_collection.json

Radhika Kandoori Ana Oliva (contractor) Christopher Ariel Valenzuela Medina
Testing runbook and Postman collections - remember, you will need to adjust urls to your testing environment
Andy Holtsclaw

Andy Holtsclaw Jason Thomas (Jason, I can run with this with Andy but figured you needed to know it is going on …)

FR2 coverage gap — for PM decision (Andy Holtsclaw)

This story alerts on unmatched settlement transactions in one direction only. Flagging a gap between what the PRD/epic/story claim (FR2) and what the code actually does.

What we found

  • eMAF orphan (FR1) — settlement-file record with no matching Payments record: ✅ detected and surfaced in the alert.
  • Payments orphan (FR2) — a Payments record that never appears in a settlement file: ❌ no producer exists anywhere. Every writer of the UnmatchedTransaction table (ADF settlement procs: Sales Settlement, Refund Settlement; and the four treasury-ms C# loaders: Funding Instruction Confirmation, ChargeBack, Funding Instruction Reject, ChargeBack Core) only ever records "settlement present, payment missing."

Removed, or never built? Never built. PAY-3605 In Stage is alert-only — it reads the whole table and emails it. It scoped FR1 and FR2 detection as pre-existing ("verified in story"). Pre-dev verification (2026-06-24) disproved that for FR2, but the PRD/epic/story were never corrected. The 2026-06-25 change that dropped the two-section eMAF/Payments email layout was presentation only — not detection.

Already raised, still open. decision-request-d2-aggregation-strategy.md Q3: "We found no writer that produces a true Payments orphan… FR2 may be unimplementable as written. Options: descope FR2, or identify the source." Marked "resolved by a single flat list" — but that resolved the email layout, not the missing data source.

Residual risk: a payment we expect to be funded/settled that never lands in a Worldpay settlement file goes unnoticed — the exact gap this feature was meant to close, closed in one direction only.

What the PM needs to resolve

Is FR2 a real operational need, or was FR1 the true requirement?

If not needed → formally descope FR2 and reconcile the PRD / epic FR-map / this story (low effort).

If needed → it's net-new work (a payments→settlement reconciliation pass); open a new story. Should not block PAY-3605 In Stage from shipping. Product to define which payments are "expected to settle" and the tolerance window before one counts as orphaned.

Suggested next step: confirm with Ops/reconciliation whether "a payment we sent that Worldpay never settled" is something they chase today. That answer decides descope vs. new story.

Full write-up: _bmad-output/products/payments/03_Features/unmatched-settlement-transaction-alerts/01_Analysis/fr2-coverage-gap-summary.md
